world_overview.name: Atrium and Ash
world_overview.description: Centuries after the Great War, surface towns run on civic debate and plant medicine while relic hunters descend into buried cyberpunk cities for chips and machines no one can make anymore. Public baths remain a ritual of cleanliness—and a way to spot implants.
world_overview.timePeriod: Post-collapse epoch, several centuries after the Great War
world_overview.technologyLevel: Modern surface society salvaging from buried futuristic ruins
world_overview.magicSystem: No magic; advanced ancients' tech can appear supernatural
world_overview.history: The Great War ended with massive urban collapses. Cyberpunk megacities were buried beneath earth and rubble. Survivors built new societies emphasizing civic discourse, natural medicine, and cautious interaction with the ruins. The Relictors' Code emerged to govern salvage operations.
world_overview.rulesPhysics: No magic. Residual Syndrome (RS) from legacy chemicals/nanotech/biotech exposure. Relictors' Code enforces: log finds, no abandonment, mandatory baths after descent, no black-market deals before guild registration.

places.name: Verdancia
places.description: Market town built atop the Fallen Grid ruins. Forum Hill rises on the north bank of the Verdan River, with the Public Baths and Atrium Relictorum within 300-500m. Two oxbow lakes (Old Weirs) lie to the south. The city center serves as hub for relic hunting operations.
places.government: Contio (open assembly), Collegium Relictorum (guild authority), Symposium (scholarly council)
places.economy: Grain, botanicals, clean water, scrap ferrous; relies on salvaged tech (processors, energy cells, clean solvents) from ruins below
places.culture: Civic debate culture through Contio and Disputatio. Public baths are mandatory civic ritual for hunters. Mixed attitudes toward relic hunters (admired for courage, shunned as contaminated). Religious diversity with significant skeptic population.
places.defenses: Stewards of the Perimeter enforce writ compliance and conduct bath inspections
notableFeatures: Forum Hill - city center with Contio square
Public Baths - mandatory inspection site for hunters
Atrium Relictorum - guildhall for writs, tithes, and crew rivalry
West Shaft - primary ruin access point with CO₂ sumps and coolant fog
Freight Lift (sealed) - deep access point discovered mid-Season 1
The Fallen Grid beneath - buried transit hub and data centers

places.name: Kestrel Ford
places.description: River market town 38km from Verdancia, accessible via 2-day barge and road journey. Access point to Finance Data Mezzanine ruins with intact UPS bricks.
places.economy: River fish, intact UPS bricks (partial), energy cells (partial); trade hub for barge traffic
places.culture: Trade-focused, less politically charged than Verdancia
notableFeatures: Finance Data Mezzanine - ruin access with brittle UPS bricks
Barge docks for Verdan River trade

places.name: Ember Gate
places.description: Border hamlet 56km from Verdancia via 3-day ridge trail. Access point to defense bunkers with structural alloys and ceramics.
places.government: Ember Gate council (disputes jurisdiction with Verdancia Collegium)
places.economy: Structural alloys, ceramics, fasteners from collapsed bunkers; scarce in processors, medicinals, potable water
notableFeatures: Patrol Corridor - unstable maintenance tunnel with war-machine echoes

places.name: StoneSpan Waystation
places.description: Checkpoint and depot 60km from Verdancia via 2-day road journey. Connects road network to sunrail spur.

places.name: Civitas Aurelia
places.description: Federation capital 190km from Verdancia (5 days via StoneSpan, 8-10 days overland). Major Symposium and Collegium offices.
places.economy: Administrative center, advanced fabrication, trade nexus

characters.name: Cassian Mire
characters.description: Wiry farm mechanic with calloused hands and observant eyes
characters.personality: Quiet observer, morally grounded, curious to a fault
characters.backstory: Raised on an orchard commune repairing pumps and mills. Father died after a black-market nanocure went wrong. Joined to salvage chips for failing irrigation back home.
characters.motivation: Save his commune's irrigation system; prevent others from suffering his father's fate
fears: Losing crew members like he lost his father
Making technical mistakes that cost lives

characters.name: Dorian Vale
characters.description: Stoic ex-Steward enforcer with military bearing; strong enough to fight, haunted by those he couldn't save
characters.personality: Disciplined, guilt-ridden, protective to a fault
characters.backstory: Survived a collapse that killed his team; left the Stewards when ordered to abandon trapped colleagues. Seeks redemption through protecting his new crew.
characters.motivation: Redeem himself by saving others; never abandon crew members
fears: Being forced to choose who lives and dies

characters.name: Silas Kern
characters.description: Ex-archivist apprentice with nervous energy and keen eyes for data patterns
characters.personality: Clever, restless, conspiracy-prone, tempted by validation
characters.backstory: Expelled from Symposium for dangerous methods; knows Grey Merchants by first name. Obsessed with relic data and what secrets it contains.
characters.motivation: Uncover hidden knowledge; prove his theories correct; gain recognition

relationships.description: Grey Merchant broker who offers tempting deals

characters.name: Maris Thorn
characters.description: Rootbound-raised mapper who treats the ruins like hostile ecosystems; calm and methodical
characters.personality: Calm, observant, judgmental about tech use
characters.backstory: Trained by Verdant Creed to detect collapse and bad air; joined to test her belief that tech is poison while helping crew survive.
characters.motivation: Prove ancient tech's dangers; protect crew from contamination; map the truth
weaknesses: Distrust of relics delays salvage operations
Rigid ethics create tension under pressure

characters.name: Liora Senn
characters.description: Soft-spoken master of trading up; unreadable and surgical with favors
characters.personality: Polite, unreadable, surgical with favors, strategic
characters.backstory: Ex-Collegium clerk turned dealmaker; maps what everyone wants and what they'll trade for it. Builds complex webs of obligation and favor.
characters.motivation: Build influence through information and deals; ensure crew prosperity
fears: Being forced to choose between profit and loyalty
weaknesses: Grows quiet enemies who see through her tactics
Distrust builds when her 'luck' is recognized as skill

npcs.name: Prefect Amara Dekk
npcs.description: Stewards commander responsible for bath inspections and writ enforcement. Has a secret: her cousin trades with the Grey network.
npcs.personality: Professional, strict but fair, conflicted by family ties

npcs.name: Vessel
npcs.description: Enigmatic Grey Merchant broker who handles quiet buyouts and smuggling routes. Once worked as a Collegium auditor.

npcs.name: Cantor Lume
npcs.description: Ascendant religious leader who conducts sacred relic deals and implant rites. Hides implant rejection with cosmetics.

npcs.name: Orator Sere
npcs.description: Charismatic Flow movement leader who organizes drum circles and forum speeches against relic hunting. Secretly keeps a private stash of nanocures.

npcs.name: Archivist Nen
npcs.description: Scholarly assessor who certifies salvage and coaches debate. Secretly protects a banned research cache.

factions.name: Collegium Relictorum
factions.description: Official guild governing relic salvage operations. Issues writs, collects tithes, enforces the Relictors' Code, and certifies finds.
goals: Control and regulate all salvage operations
factions.methods: Legal authority, permits, inspections, tithe enforcement, bath requirements
factions.leadership: Council of senior members, regional prefects
factions.resources: Legal authority, tithe revenue, inspection infrastructure
factions.reputation: Necessary but bureaucratic; respected and resented

factions.name: Stewards of the Perimeter
factions.description: Military/police force responsible for perimeter security, writ enforcement, and bath inspections.
factions.methods: Patrols, inspections, enforcement actions, military discipline
factions.resources: Military equipment, legal authority, garrison network

factions.name: The Flow
factions.description: Nature-focused movement believing intervention breeds hubris. Conducts bath rituals and drum-circle protests. Many members are hypocrites who quietly use relic cures.
factions.methods: Peaceful protests, drum circles, public shaming, ritual baths
factions.resources: Popular support, herbal knowledge, moral authority
factions.reputation: Idealistic but hypocritical; peaceful but annoying

factions.name: The Ascendants
factions.description: Belief system treating the ancients as godlike and relics as sacraments. Love-hate relationship with hunters: call them defilers yet try to buy artifacts before guild intake. Hide implants to avoid bath exposure.
factions.methods: Secret implant installations, pre-guild buyouts, religious ceremonies, bath evasion
factions.resources: Wealth from elite membership, underground medical facilities

relationships.description: Secret partnerships for implants and pre-guild purchases

factions.name: Verdant Creed (Rootbound)
factions.description: Earth-mother reverence movement believing ancient tech is sinful and ruins should be sealed. Most are peaceful; a few extremists sabotage expeditions.
goals: Protect the earth from ancient sins
factions.methods: Peaceful advocacy, education, occasional sabotage by extremists
factions.resources: Herbal knowledge, farming expertise, moral conviction
factions.reputation: Peaceful idealists with dangerous extremist fringe

factions.name: Grey Merchants
factions.description: Black-market network trading in relics, nanocures, and implants. Offers routes to bypass guild tithes. Connected to corrupt officials and desperate buyers.
factions.methods: Smuggling, bribery, pre-guild buyouts, underground medical services
factions.resources: Hidden wealth, smuggling routes, corrupt contacts

relationships.description: Supply implants and relics to wealthy members

religions.name: The Flow
religions.description: Philosophical movement believing nature should take its course and intervention breeds hubris.
beliefs: Ancient tech caused the Great War

religions.name: The Ascendants
religions.description: Belief system treating the ancients as godlike entities. Relics are sacraments; augmentation leads to ascension.
holyDays: Day of First Light (commemorating ancient cities)
religions.symbols: Circuit patterns, implant marks, neon blue

religions.name: Verdant Creed
religions.description: Earth-mother reverence. Ancient tech is sinful; ruins should be sealed to protect the world.

terms.definition: Open public assembly where expeditions are debated and writs are granted
terms.usage: The crew presented their case at the Contio

terms.definition: Formal, rules-based debate used to challenge permits or ethics
terms.usage: The rival crew called for Disputatio over the writ

terms.definition: Scholarly council that certifies finds and integrates safe tech
terms.usage: The Symposium must verify any salvaged processors

terms.definition: Official permit to enter a ruin section under defined terms and tithe
terms.usage: Without a writ, descent is illegal

terms.definition: Guild rules: mark finds, no abandonment, mandatory bath, no black-market before guild
terms.usage: Breaking the Relictors' Code means losing your license

terms.definition: Chronic illness from legacy chemicals/nanotech/biotech exposure; abbreviated RS
terms.usage: Many hunters develop RS after years of descent work

terms.definition: Black-market brokers of relics, nanocures, and implants
terms.etymology: Named for operating in legal grey areas
terms.usage: The Grey Merchants offered double the guild price

terms.definition: Hunter who evades post-descent inspection to hide contamination or implants
terms.usage: The Candle Rats are notorious bathdodgers

terms.definition: Hunter who abandoned crew members in the ruins
terms.etymology: Oath-breaker who left others in shadow
terms.usage: The Iron Dregs are whispered to be Shadowsworn

terms.definition: Buried megacities beneath surface towns; transit hubs, datacenters, biotech vaults
terms.etymology: Power grid that fell with the cities
terms.usage: The West Shaft descends into the Fallen Grid

terms.definition: Guildhall tavern and registry for relic hunters; writ posting and crew rivalry hub
terms.usage: The crew gathered at the Atrium to review writs

terms.definition: Military/police force enforcing perimeter security and salvage laws
terms.usage: Stewards conduct bath inspections after every descent

items.name: Lithography Mask Set
items.description: Complete set of photolithography masks for chip fabrication. Extremely fragile and valuable.
items.properties: Enables semiconductor fabrication; requires clean-room environment; contamination risk if mishandled

items.name: Cold Sterilizer Unit
items.description: Autonomous sterilization unit for cleaning chips and circuit boards. May contain active nanobots.
items.properties: Cleans electronic components; nano residue exposure risk

items.name: UPS Brick (intact)
items.description: Uninterruptible power supply unit, partially charged. Old cells risk thermal runaway.
items.properties: Provides short-duration power; thermal runaway hazard if damaged

items.name: Encrypted Data Shard
items.description: Storage device containing encrypted archives or access keys. May trigger alarms when activated.
items.properties: Contains valuable data; boot may trigger security systems

items.name: Filter Media Roll
items.description: Industrial air filtration material for ventilation system retrofits. Can trap fine toxins.
items.properties: Improves air quality; traps hazardous particles

items.name: Composite Shield
items.description: Vanguard's load-bearing shield made from salvaged materials

items.name: Breacher Bar
items.description: Heavy prying tool for forcing doors and clearing obstacles
items.properties: Grants advantage on forcing locked/stuck doors

items.name: P100 Respirator
items.properties: Protects against airborne hazards; requires filter replacement

items.name: LIDAR Puck
items.description: Laser-based mapping device for creating 3D structural scans
items.properties: Creates accurate spatial maps; requires power

items.name: Write Blocker
items.description: Device preventing accidental data modification during extraction
items.properties: Enables safe data imaging; prevents write operations
